draft2

Fredy Guevara

2026-04-16

# Load dataset
load("smallerdata.Rdata")


# Clean and sample data
set.seed(2401)
gaming <- gaming_data %>%
  select(
    age, gender, income, bmi, daily_gaming_hours, weekly_sessions,
    years_gaming, eye_strain_score, back_pain_score, sleep_hours,
    exercise_hours, happiness_score, aggression_score, loneliness_score,
    stress_level, anxiety_score, depression_score,
    social_interaction_score, addiction_level, toxic_exposure,
    violent_games_ratio, parental_supervision,
    microtransactions_spending, competitive_rank
  ) %>%
  drop_na() %>%
  slice_sample(n = 500000) %>%
  mutate(
    gaming_level = case_when(
      daily_gaming_hours < 3 ~ "Low",
      daily_gaming_hours < 6 ~ "Moderate",
      daily_gaming_hours < 10 ~ "High",
      TRUE ~ "Extreme"
    ),
    gaming_level = factor(gaming_level, levels = c("Low", "Moderate", "High", "Extreme")),
    
    age_group = case_when(
      age < 18 ~ "Under 18",
      age <= 25 ~ "18-25",
      age <= 40 ~ "25-40",
      TRUE ~ "40+"
    ),
    age_group = factor(age_group, levels = c("Under 18", "18-25", "25-40", "40+")),
    
    mental_health_score = (stress_level + anxiety_score + depression_score) / 3
  )


glimpse(gaming)
## Rows: 500,000
## Columns: 27
## $ age                        <dbl> 49, 19, 47, 14, 30, 51, 48, 56, 15, 17, 20,…
## $ gender                     <chr> "Male", "Female", "Female", "Female", "Male…
## $ income                     <dbl> 5821, 57352, 53992, 61017, 11158, 43559, 10…
## $ bmi                        <dbl> 23.93, 23.35, 22.52, 29.62, 25.88, 27.96, 2…
## $ daily_gaming_hours         <dbl> 6.09, 9.79, 1.26, 5.00, 5.51, 0.40, 3.18, 1…
## $ weekly_sessions            <dbl> 5, 29, 3, 18, 39, 25, 19, 24, 20, 34, 29, 3…
## $ years_gaming               <dbl> 23, 8, 20, 23, 16, 18, 9, 10, 21, 6, 13, 0,…
## $ eye_strain_score           <dbl> 2.70, 3.31, 5.13, 6.20, 6.14, 5.04, 3.98, 3…
## $ back_pain_score            <dbl> 3.80, 2.51, 4.75, 4.59, 3.72, 8.24, 5.98, 2…
## $ sleep_hours                <dbl> 6.15, 4.78, 8.01, 7.72, 10.02, 6.94, 7.08, …
## $ exercise_hours             <dbl> 1.45, 1.20, 1.75, 0.38, 2.58, 0.32, 0.03, 3…
## $ happiness_score            <dbl> 8.79, 4.28, 7.38, 4.75, 5.37, 6.21, 4.11, 9…
## $ aggression_score           <dbl> 3.04, 4.77, 5.07, 7.69, 5.81, 7.90, 6.01, 3…
## $ loneliness_score           <dbl> 8.14, 6.37, 5.85, 4.99, 4.64, 2.97, 2.97, 4…
## $ stress_level               <dbl> 10, 1, 9, 3, 8, 9, 3, 1, 4, 6, 1, 2, 3, 7, …
## $ anxiety_score              <dbl> 4.07, 6.31, 5.39, 5.25, 7.99, 3.79, 2.58, 6…
## $ depression_score           <dbl> 3.94, 6.26, 8.56, 10.00, 1.58, 4.50, 1.99, …
## $ social_interaction_score   <dbl> 5.39, 6.51, 5.54, 5.64, 5.00, 6.99, 3.15, 8…
## $ addiction_level            <dbl> 3.32, 8.71, 1.56, 3.70, 2.98, 0.82, 2.15, 1…
## $ toxic_exposure             <dbl> 0.18, 0.51, 0.28, 0.45, 0.23, 0.32, 0.22, 0…
## $ violent_games_ratio        <dbl> 0.47, 0.44, 0.44, 0.78, 0.72, 0.31, 0.57, 0…
## $ parental_supervision       <dbl> 6, 7, 10, 6, 3, 2, 3, 0, 1, 8, 7, 9, 3, 0, …
## $ microtransactions_spending <dbl> 1004.44, 451.74, 711.08, 240.58, 50.77, 76.…
## $ competitive_rank           <dbl> 91, 78, 50, 6, 33, 99, 58, 69, 43, 54, 31, …
## $ gaming_level               <fct> High, High, Low, Moderate, Moderate, Low, M…
## $ age_group                  <fct> 40+, 18-25, 40+, Under 18, 25-40, 40+, 40+,…
## $ mental_health_score        <dbl> 6.003333, 4.523333, 7.650000, 6.083333, 5.8…

Physical Effects of Gaming

Does more gaming increase eye strain?

ggplot(gaming, aes(x = daily_gaming_hours, y = eye_strain_score)) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE) +
  labs(
    title = "Gaming Hours vs Eye Strain",
    x = "Daily Gaming Hours",
    y = "Eye Strain Score"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

ggplot(gaming, aes(x = daily_gaming_hours, y = eye_strain_score)) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE)

ggplot(gaming, aes(x = gaming_level, y = eye_strain_score, fill = gaming_level)) +
  geom_boxplot()

ggplot(gaming, aes(x = gaming_level, y = eye_strain_score, fill = gaming_level)) +
  geom_violin()

ggplot(gaming, aes(x = daily_gaming_hours, y = eye_strain_score)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.2) +
  labs(title = "Gaming Hours vs Eye Strain",
       x = "Daily Gaming Hours",
       y = "Eye Strain Score") +
  theme_minimal()

gaming %>%
  group_by(gaming_level) %>%
  summarise(avg_eye_strain = mean(eye_strain_score, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = gaming_level, y = avg_eye_strain)) +
  geom_col() +
  labs(title = "Average Eye Strain by Gaming Level",
       x = "Gaming Level",
       y = "Average Eye Strain Score") +
  theme_minimal()

Can gaming behavior predict depression?

depression_model <- lm(
  depression_score ~ daily_gaming_hours + sleep_hours +
    exercise_hours + social_interaction_score + addiction_level,
  data = gaming
)

summary(depression_model)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = depression_score ~ daily_gaming_hours + sleep_hours + 
##     exercise_hours + social_interaction_score + addiction_level, 
##     data = gaming)
## 
## Residuals:
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -5.0322 -1.6896  0.0023  1.6902  5.0364 
## 
## Coefficients:
##                            Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)               5.023e+00  2.013e-02 249.532   <2e-16 ***
## daily_gaming_hours       -3.655e-03  2.643e-03  -1.383   0.1667    
## sleep_hours               1.446e-05  2.264e-03   0.006   0.9949    
## exercise_hours           -1.276e-04  1.698e-03  -0.075   0.9401    
## social_interaction_score -3.244e-03  1.732e-03  -1.873   0.0611 .  
## addiction_level           5.237e-03  3.540e-03   1.479   0.1391    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 2.401 on 499994 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  1.143e-05,  Adjusted R-squared:  1.434e-06 
## F-statistic: 1.143 on 5 and 499994 DF,  p-value: 0.3347
ggplot(gaming, aes(x = depression_score, y = predict(depression_model))) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3) +
  labs(title = "Actual vs Predicted Depression",
       x = "Actual",
       y = "Predicted")

lm(depression_score ~ loneliness_score + social_interaction_score + addiction_level, data = gaming)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = depression_score ~ loneliness_score + social_interaction_score + 
##     addiction_level, data = gaming)
## 
## Coefficients:
##              (Intercept)          loneliness_score  social_interaction_score  
##                5.0274969                -0.0013941                -0.0032402  
##          addiction_level  
##                0.0008728

Trying to detect high addiction users

gaming <- gaming %>%
  mutate(high_addiction = ifelse(addiction_level > 7, 1, 0))

model_addiction <- glm(
  high_addiction ~ daily_gaming_hours + sleep_hours + loneliness_score,
  data = gaming,
  family = "binomial"
)

summary(model_addiction)
## 
## Call:
## glm(formula = high_addiction ~ daily_gaming_hours + sleep_hours + 
##     loneliness_score, family = "binomial", data = gaming)
## 
## Coefficients:
##                      Estimate Std. Error  z value Pr(>|z|)    
## (Intercept)        -13.171341   0.096900 -135.928   <2e-16 ***
## daily_gaming_hours   1.317842   0.008022  164.278   <2e-16 ***
## sleep_hours          0.002416   0.007771    0.311    0.756    
## loneliness_score    -0.004503   0.005874   -0.767    0.443    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## (Dispersion parameter for binomial family taken to be 1)
## 
##     Null deviance: 191045  on 499999  degrees of freedom
## Residual deviance:  51282  on 499996  degrees of freedom
## AIC: 51290
## 
## Number of Fisher Scoring iterations: 9

gaming -> sleep -> depression

model_sleep <- lm(sleep_hours ~ daily_gaming_hours, data = gaming)
model_depression <- lm(depression_score ~ sleep_hours, data = gaming)

summary(model_sleep)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = sleep_hours ~ daily_gaming_hours, data = gaming)
## 
## Residuals:
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -7.1719 -1.0119 -0.0014  1.0113  6.9794 
## 
## Coefficients:
##                      Estimate Std. Error  t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)         7.0028764  0.0036736 1906.282   <2e-16 ***
## daily_gaming_hours -0.0003442  0.0007492   -0.459    0.646    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 1.499 on 499998 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  4.223e-07,  Adjusted R-squared:  -1.578e-06 
## F-statistic: 0.2111 on 1 and 499998 DF,  p-value: 0.6459
summary(model_depression)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = depression_score ~ sleep_hours, data = gaming)
## 
## Residuals:
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -5.0037 -1.6936  0.0064  1.6864  4.9965 
## 
## Coefficients:
##              Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept) 5.004e+00  1.621e-02 308.593   <2e-16 ***
## sleep_hours 9.531e-06  2.264e-03   0.004    0.997    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 2.401 on 499998 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  3.543e-11,  Adjusted R-squared:  -2e-06 
## F-statistic: 1.771e-05 on 1 and 499998 DF,  p-value: 0.9966

Clustering Gamers

cluster_data <- gaming %>%
  select(daily_gaming_hours, addiction_level, loneliness_score, sleep_hours) %>%
  scale()

set.seed(123)
kmeans_result <- kmeans(cluster_data, centers = 3)

gaming$cluster <- as.factor(kmeans_result$cluster)

gaming <- gaming %>%
  mutate(cluster_label = case_when(
    cluster == 1 ~ "Isolated Casual",
    cluster == 2 ~ "Healthy Casual",
    cluster == 3 ~ "High Risk"
  ))

gaming$cluster_label <- factor(
  gaming$cluster_label,
  levels = c("Healthy Casual", "Isolated Casual", "High Risk")
)

gaming %>%
  group_by(cluster) %>%
  summarise(
    avg_hours = mean(daily_gaming_hours),
    avg_addiction = mean(addiction_level),
    avg_sleep = mean(sleep_hours),
    avg_loneliness = mean(loneliness_score)
  )
## # A tibble: 3 × 5
##   cluster avg_hours avg_addiction avg_sleep avg_loneliness
##   <fct>       <dbl>         <dbl>     <dbl>          <dbl>
## 1 1            2.81          1.94      6.93           6.63
## 2 2            2.80          1.93      7.07           3.37
## 3 3            8.04          5.82      7.00           4.99
ggplot(gaming, aes(x = daily_gaming_hours, y = addiction_level, color = cluster_label)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5) +
  labs(title = "Gamer Clusters",
       x = "Daily Gaming Hours",
       y = "Addiction Level",
       color = "Gamer Type") +
  theme_minimal()

How does mental health vary across gaming behavior?

#grouped bar chart
gaming %>%
  group_by(gaming_level) %>%
  summarise(
    Anxiety = mean(anxiety_score),
    Depression = mean(depression_score),
    Stress = mean(stress_level),
    Happiness = mean(happiness_score)
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(-gaming_level) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = gaming_level, y = value, fill = name)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge") +
  labs(title = "Mental Health by Gaming Level",
       x = "Gaming Level",
       y = "Average Score",
       fill = "Metric") +
  theme_minimal()

#Distribution
ggplot(gaming, aes(x = depression_score)) +
  geom_histogram() +
  facet_wrap(~ gaming_level)

# The distributions of depression scores remain nearly identical across all gaming levels, with similar centers and spreads. This suggests that increased gaming time does not significantly impact depression in this dataset.

# Boxplot
ggplot(gaming, aes(x = gaming_level, y = depression_score, fill = gaming_level)) +
  geom_boxplot()

# Cluster comparison
ggplot(gaming, aes(x = cluster_label, y = depression_score, fill = cluster_label)) +
  geom_boxplot()

# Density Plot
ggplot(gaming, aes(x = depression_score, fill = gaming_level)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.4)

#Density Plot option 2
ggplot(gaming, aes(x = depression_score)) +
  geom_density(fill = "steelblue", alpha = 0.6) +
  facet_wrap(~ gaming_level) +
  labs(title = "Depression Distribution by Gaming Level",
       x = "Depression Score",
       y = "Density") +
  theme_minimal()

#Density Plot only Outline
ggplot(gaming, aes(x = depression_score, color = gaming_level)) +
  geom_density(size = 1) +
  labs(title = "Depression Distribution by Gaming Level",
       x = "Depression Score",
       y = "Density") +
  theme_minimal()

#Histogram
ggplot(gaming, aes(x = depression_score)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 1, fill = "skyblue") +
  facet_wrap(~ gaming_level) +
  labs(title = "Depression Scores by Gaming Level",
       x = "Depression Score",
       y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal()

# Addiction classification probablity 
ggplot(gaming, aes(x = daily_gaming_hours, y = high_addiction)) +
  geom_smooth(method = "glm", method.args = list(family = "binomial"))

#Bar chart


coef_df <- tidy(model_addiction) %>%
  filter(term != "(Intercept)") %>%
  mutate(odds_ratio = exp(estimate))

ggplot(coef_df, aes(x = term, y = odds_ratio)) +
  geom_col() +
  labs(title = "Effect on High Addiction Risk",
       x = "Variable",
       y = "Odds Ratio") +
  theme_minimal()

How does gaming relate to physical health outcomes?

#Boxplot
ggplot(gaming, aes(x = gaming_level, y = eye_strain_score, fill = gaming_level)) +
  geom_boxplot()

# Violin plot
ggplot(gaming, aes(x = gaming_level, y = eye_strain_score, fill = gaming_level)) +
  geom_violin()

#Histogram by gaming level
ggplot(gaming, aes(x = eye_strain_score)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 1) +
  facet_wrap(~ gaming_level)

#Heatmap
gaming %>%
  group_by(gaming_level) %>%
  summarise(
    eye = mean(eye_strain_score),
    sleep = mean(sleep_hours),
    exercise = mean(exercise_hours)
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(-gaming_level) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = name, y = gaming_level, fill = value)) +
  geom_tile()

#sleep vs gaming
ggplot(gaming, aes(x = gaming_level, y = sleep_hours, fill = gaming_level)) +
  geom_boxplot()

#Cluster physical comparison
ggplot(gaming, aes(x = cluster_label, y = sleep_hours, fill = cluster_label)) +
  geom_boxplot()

How does gaming behavior relate to social well-being?

#loneliness vs cluster
ggplot(gaming, aes(x = cluster_label, y = loneliness_score, fill = cluster_label)) +
  geom_boxplot()

#social interaction vs gaming level
ggplot(gaming, aes(x = gaming_level, y = social_interaction_score, fill = gaming_level)) +
  geom_boxplot()

# Density plot
ggplot(gaming, aes(x = loneliness_score, fill = gaming_level)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.4)

#Parental supervision
gaming %>%
  filter(age < 18) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = parental_supervision, y = daily_gaming_hours)) +
  geom_smooth()

# Cluster social comparison
ggplot(gaming, aes(x = cluster_label, y = social_interaction_score, fill = cluster_label)) +
  geom_boxplot()
